edges.description: Disinhibition of BMP/TGF-beta-SMAD signaling in osteoblast lineage cells leads to dysregulated bone maturation and formation of focal osteosclerotic bone islands.
edges.source: LEMD3/MAN1 Loss-of-Function and Dysregulated TGF-beta/BMP Signaling

edges.description: Excessive TGF-beta signaling in fibroblasts may drive fibroproliferative and stenosing connective tissue changes in the nasolacrimal drainage system.
edges.source: LEMD3/MAN1 Loss-of-Function and Dysregulated TGF-beta/BMP Signaling

edges.description: Disinhibited TGF-beta signaling in fibroblasts drives palmar fibromatosis as part of the generalized fibroproliferative phenotype.
edges.source: LEMD3/MAN1 Loss-of-Function and Dysregulated TGF-beta/BMP Signaling

edges.description: Excessive TGF-beta-SMAD signaling promotes fibroblast proliferation and abnormal scar collagen deposition.
edges.source: LEMD3/MAN1 Loss-of-Function and Dysregulated TGF-beta/BMP Signaling

edges.description: Stenosing tenosynovitis (trigger finger) results from fibroproliferative thickening of tendon sheaths driven by dysregulated TGF-beta signaling.
edges.source: LEMD3/MAN1 Loss-of-Function and Dysregulated TGF-beta/BMP Signaling

edges.description: The focal osteosclerotic bone islands are the radiographic phenotype described clinically as osteopoikilosis.
The osteosclerotic bone islands of osteopoikilosis represent focal areas of increased bone mineral density.
Joint pain occurs in 15-20% of patients with osteopoikilosis, potentially related to periarticular osteosclerotic lesions.
Joint effusion occasionally accompanies osteopoikilosis lesions.
Stenosis of the lacrimal sac or nasolacrimal canal is represented clinically as lacrimal duct stenosis.
Lacrimal duct stenosis causes tear stasis and secondary infection of the lacrimal sac.
Obstructed tear drainage leads to overflow tearing.

edges.target: LEMD3/MAN1 Loss-of-Function and Dysregulated TGF-beta/BMP Signaling

nodes.description: Joint pain reported in 15-20% of osteopoikilosis patients, sometimes accompanied by joint swelling or effusion.

evidence.reference_title: Osteopoikilosis: report of a familial case and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: Although it is usually asymptomatic, effusion and joint pain can be found in 15-20 % of patients.

nodes.description: Chronic inflammation of the lacrimal sac due to nasolacrimal duct or lacrimal canal stenosis. Confirmed in the original family by lacrimal lavage and dacryocystography.

evidence.snippet: We report five members of a family with dacryocystitis associated with osteopoikilosis. The inheritance is autosomal dominant.

nodes.description: Stenosing tenosynovitis (trigger finger) reported in the Gunal family as part of the fibroproliferative spectrum of osteopoikilosis.

evidence.reference_title: Disorders associated with osteopoikilosis: 5 different lesions in a family.
evidence.snippet: We report a family in whom various members had osteopoikilosis with 5 different associated lesions. We suggest that osteopoikilosis is a bone manifestation of a generalized fibroproliferative or stenosing disease.

nodes.description: Palmar fibromatosis leading to digital flexion contracture, reported in the same family with osteopoikilosis as part of a generalized fibroproliferative tendency.

evidence.reference_title: Disorders associated with osteopoikilosis: 5 different lesions in a family.
evidence.snippet: We report a family in whom various members had osteopoikilosis with 5 different associated lesions. We suggest that osteopoikilosis is a bone manifestation of a generalized fibroproliferative or stenosing disease.

nodes.description: Overflow of tears due to impaired drainage through the stenosed nasolacrimal duct, a direct consequence of the lacrimal canal obstruction that characterizes this syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Acquired nasolacrimal duct obstruction: clinical and histological findings of 275 cases.
evidence.snippet: Epiphora with continuous purulent discharge was the most common clinical sign reported by 144 (52.4%) patients

nodes.description: Multiple small, discrete, symmetric periarticular osteosclerotic bone lesions (2-10mm diameter) distributed in the epiphyses and metaphyses of long bones, carpal and tarsal bones, and pelvis. These lesions represent areas of focal defective endochondral bone maturation with osteosclerotic trabeculae.

evidence.reference_title: Deactivating germline mutations in LEMD3 cause osteopoikilosis and Buschke-Ollendorff syndrome, but not sporadic melorheostosis.
evidence.snippet: OPK is an autosomal dominant, usually benign, skeletal dysplasia featuring multiple, small, especially metaphyseal, oval or round, dense trabecular foci distributed symmetrically throughout the skeleton.

evidence.reference_title: Osteopoikilosis: report of a familial case and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: Osteopoikilosis (OPK) is a benign, rare, asymptomatic osteosclerotic bone dysplasia which is inherited as an autosomal dominant trait.

nodes.description: Osteosclerotic lesions characteristic of osteopoikilosis, representing focal areas of increased bone density visible on plain radiographs.

evidence.reference_title: Osteopoikilosis: report of a familial case and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: Osteopoikilosis (OPK) is a benign, rare, asymptomatic osteosclerotic bone dysplasia which is inherited as an autosomal dominant trait.

nodes.description: Joint swelling or effusion occasionally reported in osteopoikilosis patients, of unclear pathogenic significance.

evidence.reference_title: Osteopoikilosis: report of a familial case and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: Although it is usually asymptomatic, effusion and joint pain can be found in 15-20 % of patients.

nodes.description: Tendency toward keloid scar formation, consistent with the fibroproliferative connective tissue phenotype observed in osteopoikilosis-associated conditions.

evidence.reference_title: Disorders associated with osteopoikilosis: 5 different lesions in a family.
evidence.snippet: We report a family in whom various members had osteopoikilosis with 5 different associated lesions. We suggest that osteopoikilosis is a bone manifestation of a generalized fibroproliferative or stenosing disease.

evidence.reference_title: Loss-of-function mutations in LEMD3 result in osteopoikilosis, Buschke-Ollendorff syndrome and melorheostosis.
evidence.snippet: All the affected individuals that we investigated were heterozygous with respect to a loss-of-function mutation in LEMD3 (also called MAN1), which encodes an inner nuclear membrane protein.

evidence.reference_title: Deactivating germline mutations in LEMD3 cause osteopoikilosis and Buschke-Ollendorff syndrome, but not sporadic melorheostosis.
evidence.snippet: We confirm that OPK and BOS individuals, including those with MEL-like lesions, have heterozygous, deactivating, germline LEMD3 mutations.

nodes.description: Osteopoikilosis is caused by heterozygous loss-of-function mutations in LEMD3 (also called MAN1), which encodes an inner nuclear membrane protein. The C-terminal domain of LEMD3 binds receptor-activated SMADs and antagonizes both BMP and TGF-beta/activin signaling pathways. Loss of this antagonism leads to excessive SMAD-mediated signaling, perturbing bone formation and remodeling and producing focal osteosclerotic lesions.
nodes.id: LEMD3/MAN1 Loss-of-Function and Dysregulated TGF-beta/BMP Signaling

biological_processes: negative regulation of BMP signaling pathway
negative regulation of TGF-beta receptor signaling pathway

evidence.reference_title: Loss-of-function mutations in LEMD3 result in osteopoikilosis, Buschke-Ollendorff syndrome and melorheostosis.
evidence.snippet: All the affected individuals that we investigated were heterozygous with respect to a loss-of-function mutation in LEMD3 (also called MAN1), which encodes an inner nuclear membrane protein.

evidence.reference_title: Loss-of-function mutations in LEMD3 result in osteopoikilosis, Buschke-Ollendorff syndrome and melorheostosis.
evidence.snippet: LEMD3 interacted with BMP and activin-TGFbeta receptor-activated Smads and antagonized both signaling pathways in human cells.

evidence.reference_title: Deactivating germline mutations in LEMD3 cause osteopoikilosis and Buschke-Ollendorff syndrome, but not sporadic melorheostosis.
evidence.snippet: In 2004, others discovered that heterozygous, loss-of-function, germline mutations in the LEMD3 gene (LEMD3 or MAN1) cause both osteopoikilosis (OPK) and Buschke-Ollendorff syndrome (BOS).

nodes.description: Stenosis of the lacrimal sac or nasolacrimal canal leads to impaired tear drainage and chronic dacryocystitis. The stenosis is hypothesized to arise from fibroproliferative connective tissue changes related to the underlying LEMD3-associated connective tissue disorder, analogous to stenosing lesions seen at other sites in the osteopoikilosis spectrum.

evidence.snippet: We report five members of a family with dacryocystitis associated with osteopoikilosis. The inheritance is autosomal dominant.

nodes.description: Narrowing of the nasolacrimal duct or lacrimal canal, which is the prerequisite for the development of dacryocystitis in this syndrome.

evidence.snippet: We report five members of a family with dacryocystitis associated with osteopoikilosis. The inheritance is autosomal dominant.

nodes.description: Multiple discrete spherical osteosclerotic bone islands (2-10mm diameter) visible on radiographic examination, distributed symmetrically in periarticular locations, particularly the epiphyses and metaphyses of long bones.

evidence.snippet: We report five members of a family with dacryocystitis associated with osteopoikilosis. The inheritance is autosomal dominant.
